title: The Bear Whisperer
subTitle: Steve deals with a bear who was in an inappropriate sleeping spot; a badly wounded bear.

title: Shark Dive TV
subTitle: Sharks at Palau's Blue Corner; how sharks adapt in Denver's high-altitude aquarium.

title: Shark Dive TV
subTitle: The odd behavior of hammerhead sharks; a blue hole adventure in Belize.

title: Shark Dive TV
subTitle: Whale sharks kept in a giant tank; shark hearing, shark feeding frenzies.

title: Shark Dive TV
subTitle: Diving with bull sharks in Cancun, Mexico; sharks' electrosensory systems.

title: Wild Ones
subTitle: From pelican to chattering parrots, kestrels to eagles, there are lots of our flying, feathered friends to meet in this episode.

title: Wild Ones
subTitle: Investigates which is the biggest of them all. In the modern world, the largest land animals are native to Africa and Asia, but the oceans conceal true giants.

title: Wild Ones
subTitle: From venomous death adders unseen in dried leaves to chameleons changing color in seconds to delicate weedy sea dragons blending with the gardens of the ocean, many animals are masters of the art of camouflage.

title: Animals at Work
subTitle: Misty the owl prepares for his first ever public flight, a Hungarian sheepdog competes at the national championships and a cherished police horse celebrates his retirement at Liverpool's Lord Mayor's Parade.

title: Animals at Work
subTitle: A legendary all-cat rock band rehearses for an upcoming gig, a Labrador motivates young patients to be active, and a sheep milking contest.

title: Animals at Work
subTitle: Inti the llama trains for his first ever trek, a disabled Chihuahua brings joy to kids in Los Angeles, and an Irish hedgehog starts work as a slug munching pest controller.

title: Second Chance Pets
subTitle: A Samoyed from Maine gets a prosthetic for a missing rear paw; a dog learns how to swim again after getting a brace.

title: Second Chance Pets
subTitle: A spoiled pup from Delaware needs a brace to fix an injured back leg; a cadaver dog gets a brace to keep working.

title: Second Chance Pets
subTitle: At a rescue center in Pennsylvania a dog gets a brace to help it walk; a dog named Rigby is healing from a torn Achilles.

title: Second Chance Pets
subTitle: Frostbite causes a dog to lose both front paws; a police dog needs a brace to get back to work.

title: Blue World
subTitle: Underwater caves in Andros in the Bahamas; Georgia Aquarium in Atlanta is the largest aquarium in the world.

title: Blue World
subTitle: Majestic and unique creatures call the underground world of Palm Beach, Fla., home; oceanic secrets can be found up close and personal across the Great Barrier Reef.

title: Blue World
subTitle: A stranded pilot whale is rescued by the staff of the Curacao Sea Aquarium; animals have parasites and dead skin removed at cleaning stations.

title: Blue World
subTitle: Diving with sea lions in the Galapagos Islands; training sea lions at the Mystic Aquarium in Connecticut; humpback whales in Silver Bank, Dominican Republic.

title: Inside Animal A&E
subTitle: Behind the scenes at the Blue Cross animal hospitals in London Victoria and Grimsby.

title: Dog Dudes
subTitle: Canine Rehabilitation Experts Dylan and Darien Northstar help troubled dogs become good dogs.

title: Doug to the Rescue
subTitle: In the wake of Hurricane Laura, Doug heads to Louisiana to help a local firefighter find his lost dog and begin rescue operations in the hard-hit city of Lake Charles.

title: Modern Migrations
subTitle: With helicopter blades turning precision flying and excellent planning, viewers are taken on an adrenaline-packed ride transporting wild animals including buffalo giraffe and warthog to new habitats.

title: Modern Migrations
subTitle: When the animal to capture has a very long neck and standing almost five meters high and able to spot predators a mile away, the strategy needs to be dramatically adapted.

title: Modern Migrations
subTitle: The capture team is busy in Ithala Game Reserve where several warthogs need new pastures; an interesting small animal called a dassie' or rock hyrax is lured into traps as they are becoming a menace in the Ntshondwe camp area.

title: Modern Migrations
subTitle: Being one of Africa's Big 5, the buffalo is an animal that requires a well-experienced team to capture and load; bushbuck on the other hand proves to be much easier to capture.

title: Wild Walk
subTitle: The people that run the Cheetah Outreach in Cape Town attempt to save wild cheetahs.

title: Wild Walk
subTitle: The domestication of African elephants in Knysna Park.

title: Wild Walk
subTitle: A walk in the footsteps of the early hunter gatherers of southern Africa.

title: Wild Walk
subTitle: Explore the underwater forests and beaches of the inter tidal zones of the Cape of Storms.

title: Quolls: Fast and Furious
subTitle: Exploring the life and habits of the elusive northern subspecies of the spotted-tailed quoll.

title: Adaptable Animals
subTitle: Three-legged rescues from Mexico and Russia are cast and fitted for new prosthetic legs.

title: Adaptable Animals
subTitle: A dog and a goat meet in their front-legged carts; an elderly Labradoodle hopes to stay active with a new knee brace; a German Shepherd-Mastiff mix needs an indoor slipper.

title: Adaptable Animals
subTitle: Big changes are in store for PawsAbility as Janice moves her practice; an old friend brings new visitors for brace fittings; a former Olympic Sledge Hockey champion and his miniature dachshund get much needed cart adjustments.

title: Super Senses
subTitle: Animals use all of their senses to communicate, to attract partners, discourage predators, and deceive their prey; they send messages without needing words.

title: Super Senses
subTitle: The most spectacular senses help animals rule their environment; the champions of the animal world reveal their special weapons and abilities.

title: Monkey Thieves
subTitle: In Jaipur, India, the Galta Gang, a troop of rhesus macaques, face food shortages; venturing into the city, they must steal to survive and avoid many dangers, including the dreaded monkey catcher.

title: Monkey Thieves
subTitle: The Galta Gang are trying to invade Fang territory.

title: Monkey Thieves
subTitle: Monkey catcher called in when delayed monsoon rain and food shortages are causing monkey attacks.

subTitle: Creatures of Asia: the snow leopard; the Komodo dragon.

title: Animal Science
subTitle: The seahorse; the Thomson's gazelle.

subTitle: Scuba divers get up close to tiger sharks in the Bahamas and Australia; divers are introduced to the rare salmon shark in Alaska.

title: Blue Realm
subTitle: Manta rays in the Socorro Islands off Mexico's Pacific coast; Bubbles, a 15-foot manta in the Bahamas.

title: The Blue Realm
subTitle: Researchers hope to prove reef squid speak to each other with a language they paint on their skin; egg-laying rituals of reef squid; largest octopus.

title: Roaring with Pride
subTitle: Defying sceptics, a groundbreaking program attempts to return Africa's lion population back to its former glory.

title: Roaring with Pride
subTitle: ALERT seeks to reintroduce captive bred lions to the wild.

title: Roaring with Pride
subTitle: Meat preparation gets weird for some as volunteers make dinner time more interesting; a brave zebra stallion faces the confident lions; Wakanaka proves her predatory prowess.

title: Roaring with Pride
subTitle: ALERT seeks to return lions to the wild.

title: Alaska's Wildest City: Anchorage
subTitle: As this urban jungle continues to expand into the wilderness, encounters between people and wild Lynx are on the rise.

title: World's Wildest City: Anchorage
subTitle: As expert hunters, the Bald Eagle is perfectly adapted to life in tough arctic conditions, but in Anchorage, the secret to their survival...is trash.

title: World's Wildest City: Anchorage
subTitle: For years, at least four highly elusive wolf packs have lived and hunted on the edge of the wild city of Anchorage, Alaska; as the city spreads further and further into wolf country, the wolves are getting bolder.

title: Shamwari
subTitle: An elephant finds itself in a bad position and has stopped breathing; the team needs to push it onto its side, or it will die; watch the birth of a giraffe; the wildlife team tries to bring in a herd of antelope.

title: Shamwari
subTitle: An adult leopard is caught in a trap on a nearby farm; a bull elephant causes havoc; a hyena almost locks its jaws around a vets arm.

title: Shamwari
subTitle: The perfect 21st birthday present for Siobhan, the conservation student, turns out to be an adventure with the reserve's game capture team to find black wildebeest.

title: Shamwari
subTitle: Johan tackles an adult cheetah; baby giraffe samples leaves for the first time; rangers track predators at night; baby elephant takes a dust bath; poachers kill a rhino.

title: Orphans of the Wild
subTitle: In their biggest operation of this kind yet, Rudie and Marlice release seven cheetahs and one leopard in two separate areas of Namibia, Africa.

title: City Zoo
subTitle: The Cape Vulture breeding program is trying to ensure the endurance of this rare species.

subTitle: The chimpanzees and monkeys at the City Zoo in Pretoria, South Africa have their annual check ups.

title: City Zoo
subTitle: The serval is due for his annual health check; the crew saves a porcupine from a very sticky situation; the meerkats are given a chance to play with their food.

title: City Zoo
subTitle: Some cold-blooded residents of the zoo still strike fear in some of the world's toughest creatures.

title: Keepers of the Cape
subTitle: A look at the effects of invasive alien plants on the country's water supply; Tali looks at three initiatives, Working for Wetlands, Working for Water and the Water Neutral Scheme.

title: Killer Instincts
subTitle: Showcases the smallest yet most dangerous assassins in the animal kingdom and examines how they survive and hunt in the wild.

title: Killer Instincts
subTitle: From pumas to bald eagles to grizzly bears, witness apex predators using speed, strength and cunning to dominate their realms.

title: Rhino Orphanage
subTitle: Traumatized and lonely, Pemba is in need of a friend her own age.

title: Rhino Orphanage
subTitle: The Little Nyani is born a week premature, unable to keep up with her mother, she is sent to the rhino orphanage; raising a premature calf is a first for the orphanage.

title: Rhino Orphanage
subTitle: The poaching crisis intensifies and poachers turn their attention to orphanages; after losing her mother, four-week-old Jamila finally finds sanctuary at The Rhino Orphanage.

title: Animals at Work
subTitle: Nok the macaque monkey learns to collect coconuts, Bingo graduates as a service dog for people with disabilities and Sam the beach donkey starts work in Skegness.

title: Animals at Work
subTitle: Rufus the hawk scares pooping pigeons at Wimbledon, a New Jersey performing dog troupe raises cash for homeless pets, and an elephant artist in Thailand gets creative on canvas.

title: Second Chance Pets
subTitle: A dog named Chevy needs a brace; a test shows how much pressure dogs can put on their legs after receiving braces or prosthetics.

title: Second Chance Pets
subTitle: A dog named Leo needs a front leg brace to be able to walk; a champion show dog named Sergio needs a knee brace.

title: Second Chance Pets
subTitle: Two dogs from the same home need leg braces; a dog named Scout is missing a leg and needs a brace to combat arthritis.

title: Blue World
subTitle: Thirteen-year-old Jake explores the underwater world of Grand Cayman Island with Jonathan; dolphin program at the Curacao Sea Aquarium.

title: Blue World
subTitle: Marine reptiles in Australia and the Philippines; underwater partnerships; coral spawning.

title: Blue World
subTitle: A sinkhole in the Yucatan is more than 100 feet deep; diversity of sharks; how animals protect themselves.

title: Vet Tales
subTitle: Stacey battles to save an injured hunting dog; Millie the calf is mauled by a dog; Mark and Stacey work together to change the gender of a cat.

title: Vet Tales
subTitle: Meg the Labrador has swallowed a UFO; Chocolate the horse is suffering from asthma; Mark and Stacey compete in a chilli-eating challenge.

title: Vet Tales
subTitle: Boss the dog has a nasty injury from a possum trap; the fire brigade are called to save a cat stuck up a tree; Mark and Stacey compete in a fireman's challenge.

title: Vet Tales
subTitle: Stacey discovers Marama the poodle has breast cancer; Mark tries to save a herd of pregnant cows from miscarrying; the Vet North team dart calves amongst the dinosaurs.

title: Dog Dudes
subTitle: Dylan and Darien Northstar turn Red Zone dogs from dangerous to adorable while teaching their doggy parents along the way.

title: Nightmare Classics
subTitle: An experimental drug exposes the evil within Dr. Jekyll (Anthony Andrews). With Laura Dern.

title: Dog Dudes
subTitle: Following Canine Rehabilitation Experts Dylan and Darien Northstar, and their amazing pack of Siberian Huskies. Together, they use ancient techniques only they have mastered to help troubled dogs become good dogs.

title: Dog Dudes
subTitle: Rod and Jessie are out of options with their aggressive Australian Cattle Dog, Cooper, whose animal instincts are causing him to herd humans, not cattle; the Dog Dudes must turn this cattle dog's aggressive behavior around.

title: Wild Ones
subTitle: From the mighty King Cobra to the lethal Black Widow Spider, a look inside the world of venomous animals and how they use their poisons.

title: Wild Ones
subTitle: Of the quirky Australian pelican, the distinguishedly striped African zebra and the colossal whale that roams the great oceans, we explore which one is the mightiest migrator.

title: Race of Life
subTitle: A look at how the males and females of some species fight, which can often lead to death, and examines the brevity of their arguments.

title: Race of Life
subTitle: A look at how prey utilize defensive adaptations such as speed as an effective means to escape from various predators and survive.

title: Race of Life
subTitle: Showcasing of how avian predators and prey attack and evade each other, including horizontal speeding, turning and diving.

title: Race of Life
subTitle: A look at the crocodile and its ancestors that roamed Earth millions of years ago; how snakes rely on the heat of the sun to regulate their body temperature.

title: Animal Society
subTitle: Ancient as the dinosaurs themselves take a look at the family structure of crocodiles, turtles and snakes.

title: INWILD
subTitle: The big cats are a group of apex predators that rest firmly at the top of the food chain; lions, leopards and tigers all live uniquely with particular hunting prowess, advantages and pack hierarchy or solitary territories.

title: INWILD
subTitle: Explore the worlds of hooved animals from deer and rhinos to pigs and goats. A diverse collection of some of the most interesting animals in the world.

title: INWILD
subTitle: All fish have moveable jaws and are covered in scales, using gills to breath underwater. Many forming schools or shoals based on social structure, it's a unique group arrangement with many benefits.

title: Wild About
subTitle: A detailed journey into the life and defining traits of some of the world's most majestic animals.

title: Wild About
subTitle: Lizards have existed here long before us; surviving natural disasters and mass extinctions, they are now among the oldest animals on Earth.

title: Animal Science
subTitle: How sharks can sleep while they swim; how the arctic fox survives in extreme cold.

title: Animal Science
subTitle: How the peregrine falcon can fly at speeds over 200 mph; how the alpaca survives at altitudes above 15,000 feet.

title: Animal Science
subTitle: This episode features a variety of animals found on land, and in the sea. Find out how the badger is able to dig tunnels up to 13 feet deep. And why the Siberian tiger is the largest cat in the world.

title: Adaptable Animals
subTitle: Janice is being kept busy with a rare case of a dog who uses prosthetics on both front legs.

title: Adaptable Animals
subTitle: Things are getting tiny as Janice attempts to create a prosthetic leg for a Chihuahua; a talkative black Lab who loves to chase squirrels and an Akita with an autoimmune disease need specialized braces.

title: Adaptable Animals
subTitle: Adaptable Animals

title: Super Senses
subTitle: Many animals need a strong sense of sight to capture their prey; cats can see at night and birds of prey can catch their quarry from immense heights.

title: Super Senses
subTitle: Many animals rely on their pronounced hearing in their daily struggle for survival, such as moths escaping a bat attack; most animals have a much more sensitive ear than humans.

title: Super Senses
subTitle: Animals, like humans, depend on their sense of touch in various situations, like bears in Alaska tracking down salmon and crocodiles knowing when their prey is approaching.

title: Super Senses: Special Forces
subTitle: The mantis shrimp's visual systems; the owl's hearing; the extensive sensorimotor specializations of the elephant's trunk.
